generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance SiemensSinumerik840DCNController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Siemens SINUMERIK 840D CNC controller providing control inputs/outputs (position, speed, acceleration) and motor current IM for friction modeling"
]

// C3: Physical sensing components
instance MotorEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Motor encoder mounted directly on the motor measuring motor angle and velocity"
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorCurrentData, MotorTorqueData, AngularVelocityData
]

instance GearboxEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Secondary encoder behind the gearbox detecting gearbox rotational position and velocity"
    DTDFVocab:producedData GearboxTorqueData, GearboxAngleData
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance MotorCurrentData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorEncoder
]

instance MotorTorqueData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorEncoder
]

instance AngularVelocityData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorEncoder
]

instance GearboxTorqueData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom GearboxEncoder
]

instance GearboxAngleData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom GearboxEncoder
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance IdealBehaviorModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Simulation model representing robot's first axis behavior without friction (baseline for comparison)"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MathWorksMATLABSimulink
]

instance CoulombViscousFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MathWorks MATLAB Simulink model implementing Coulomb-Viscous friction effects on robot's first axis"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MathWorksMATLABSimulink
]

instance StribeckFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MathWorks MATLAB Simulink model implementing Stribeck friction effects for dynamic model updates"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MathWorksMATLABSimulink
]

instance SimulatedMotorOutputData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Output data from friction-inclusive simulation models (e.g., simulated motor output torque and velocity)"
    DTDFVocab:fromData AngularVelocityData, MotorCurrentData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MathWorksMATLABSimulink
]

instance SimulatedGearboxOutputData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Output data from friction-inclusive simulation models for gearbox components"
    DTDFVocab:fromData GearboxTorqueData, GearboxAngleData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MathWorksMATLABSimulink
]

instance MeasuredMotorData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series data from motor sensors (motor current IM, motor torque τM, angular velocity ωM)"
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorCurrentData, MotorTorqueData, AngularVelocityData
]

instance MeasuredGearboxData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series data from gearbox sensors (gearbox torque τG, angular velocity ωG)"
    DTDFVocab:fromData GearboxTorqueData, GearboxAngleData
]

instance FrictionModelParametersData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Dynamic parameters derived from real-time friction identification (static, kinetic, viscous friction coefficients)"
    DTDFVocab:fromData MeasuredMotorData, MeasuredGearboxData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MathWorksMATLABSimulink
]

// C11: Enablers
instance MathWorksMATLABSimulink : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Executable simulation environment for integrating friction models (Coulomb-Viscous, Stribeck) and validating their impact on motion accuracy"
    DTDFVocab:enables FrictionModelingService, RealTimeDashboardService
]

instance MQTTProtocol : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Enables real-time data streaming from robot sensors (motor current, torque, angular velocity) to the digital twin via lightweight publish-subscribe protocol"
]

instance AssetAdministrationShell : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Framework for organizing and linking physical and virtual data, ensuring traceability of data origins and computational methods"
]

instance SysMLBlockDefinitionDiagrams : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SysML models (Block Definition Diagrams, Internal Block Diagrams) defining structural and behavioral models of the robot's first axis"
]

// C6: Services
instance FrictionModelingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time integration of white-box (analytical) and black-box (data-driven) friction models to dynamically adjust motion control parameters"
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionIdentificationInsight, DynamicModelUpdateAction
]

instance RealTimeDashboardService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Unified interface displaying live data traces (motor current, torque, angular velocity) alongside simulated friction models for real-time deviation analysis"
    DTDFVocab:provides DeviationAnalysisInsight
]

instance ContinuousModelRefinementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Enables dynamic exchange of friction models during operation, leveraging traceability to update parameters based on operational data (wear, load, temperature)"
    DTDFVocab:provides AdaptiveControlAction
]

instance MultiPhysicsAwarenessService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Supports multi-physics simulations combining electrical, mechanical, and friction dynamics to enhance motion accuracy"
    DTDFVocab:provides MultiPhysicsSimulationInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance FrictionIdentificationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time detection of static, kinetic, and viscous friction in the gearbox enabling dynamic model updates"
]

instance DynamicModelUpdateAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic adjustment of friction models during operation to improve motion accuracy based on real-time operational data"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance DeviationAnalysisInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Visualization dashboard highlighting discrepancies between measured and simulated data to guide adjustments to friction models or control parameters"
]

instance AdaptiveControlAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Continuous model refinement based on operational data (wear, load variations) to ensure adaptive control for high-precision tasks"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance MultiPhysicsSimulationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Integration of electrical, mechanical, and friction dynamics simulations for comprehensive motion accuracy assessment"
]

// C1: Environment
instance RobotFirstAxisEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains SiemensSinumerik840DCNController, MotorEncoder, GearboxEncoder, MathWorksMATLABSimulink, MQTTProtocol, AssetAdministrationShell, SysMLBlockDefinitionDiagrams
]

instance MABIMAX100RobotEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains RobotFirstAxisEnvironment, IdealBehaviorModel, CoulombViscousFrictionModel, StribeckFrictionModel, FrictionModelParametersData
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The twinning time scale is **real-time and near-real-time**:
   - **Data acquisition frequency**: Sensor data (e.g., motor current, torque, angular velocity) is streamed via MQTT at **high temporal resolution**, enabling **continuous identification** of friction models during operation.
   - **Model update frequency**: Friction models are dynamically refined in **real-time** (or near-real-time) to account for operational changes (e.g., wear, tooling variations).
   - **Dashboard synchronization**: The visualization dashboard updates **instantaneously** to reflect live data and model predictions, ensuring **coherent decision-making** for motion control."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The DT constellation is structured as follows:
   - **Centralized processing**: The DT aggregates data from the robot’s **first axis sensors** (motor encoder, gearbox encoder) and processes it in a **unified dashboard** for visualization and analysis.
   - **Hierarchical models**: The DT incorporates **three-tiered models** (Engineering, Data Calculation, Simulation) as per the **Digital Shadow Reference Model (DSRM)**. Engineering models describe the robot’s structure; data calculation models derive derived values (e.g., torque, velocity); simulation models simulate friction effects.
   - **Data provenance**: Each data trace (e.g., measured motor current _I_M, simulated gearbox output _ˆr_G) is linked to its **source (sensor/processing model)** and computational method via **traceability associations** in the DSRM."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The DT operates across the following life-cycle stages:
   - **As designed**: Structural models (e.g., SysML Block Definition Diagrams) define the robot’s **first axis architecture**, including motor, gearbox, and encoder components.
   - **As manufactured**: The DT integrates **real-time sensor data** (e.g., motor current, torque, angular velocity) to model friction effects during operation.
   - **As operated**: The DT continuously refines friction models (white-box and black-box) based on **operational data** (e.g., wear, load, temperature) to improve motion accuracy in real-time."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The fidelity and validity considerations include:
   - **Verification/Validation**:
     - **Model validation**: Friction models (e.g., Coulomb-Viscous, Stribeck) are validated against **real-time sensor data** (e.g., torque, angular velocity) to ensure accuracy.
     - **Traceability**: Each data point’s origin (e.g., sensor, simulation model) is documented, enabling **auditability** of model predictions.
   - **Uncertainty and errors**:
     - **Sensor noise**: Encoder readings may introduce positional uncertainty, mitigated by comparing motor and gearbox encoders.
     - **Friction model complexity**: White-box models (analytical) are simplified to avoid overfitting, while black-box models leverage **data-driven learning** for robustness.
   - **Operational constraints**:
     - **Robot limits**: The DT respects the robot’s **±90° range** and **75°/s max speed** to ensure safe operation.
     - **Data granularity**: High-frequency sensor data (e.g., MQTT streams) enables **fine-grained friction identification** but requires **real-time processing** to avoid latency."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The DT technical connection involves:
   - **Network protocol**: **MQTT** enables **real-time data streaming** from robot sensors (motor current, torque, angular velocity) to the DT.
   - **Data exchange**: Sensor data is linked to **SysML models** (e.g., Block Definition Diagrams) via **Asset Administration Shell (AAS)** for semantic support.
   - **Simulation integration**: Simulink models (e.g., friction simulations) are connected to the DT to **compare simulated vs. measured behavior** in real-time."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The DT adheres to the following standards:
   - **Modeling**: SysML (Block Definition Diagrams, Internal Block Diagrams) for structural and behavioral modeling.
   - **Data exchange**: MQTT protocol for real-time sensor data streaming.
   - **Digital Shadow Reference Model (DSRM)**: Follows the **Digital Shadow Reference Model** (Fig. 1) for defining data traces, models, and processing components."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The DT includes safety and security considerations:
   - **Data integrity**: Traceability ensures **auditability** of data origins and computational methods, reducing risks of tampering.
   - **Operational safety**: The DT respects the robot’s **±90° range** and **75°/s max speed** to prevent mechanical stress or accidents.
   - **Remote operation**: While not explicitly detailed, the DT’s real-time processing and dashboard could enable **remote monitoring** of the robot’s first axis, provided the system is securely deployed (e.g., via MQTT with authentication)."
]
